aliases: ice cream sundae with chocolate sauce

english: ice cream sundae with chocolate sauce

ontology.foodex2_description: Ice cream and similar frozen desserts
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No FoodEx2 code exists specifically for chocolate sundae; A0FKP (ice cream and similar frozen desserts) is the closest defensible parent category anchor. A more specific code for chocolate sundae with fudge sauce is not available in FoodEx2 as of 2026.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT food concept identified for chocolate sundae as of 2026.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard chocolate sundae serving is approximately 150 g, comprising a cup of soft serve ice cream topped with hot chocolate fudge sauce.

nutrient_source.title: Chocolate Sundae — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Input record cr_id F02020003 is sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo with confidence 0.95 and review_status reviewed. All per_100g values are taken from this approved primary source without modification. Saturated fat is adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry 19270 as HPB does not report saturated fat breakdown; this adaptation is documented in notes.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — ice cream and frozen dairy desserts
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. Diabetes Care 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 40 is derived from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for ice cream (full-fat, chocolate variants, GI range 36–62, representative value ~40 for chocolate ice cream). The input record cited GI 40 with confidence 0.844, consistent with the published GI range for chocolate ice cream in Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care). Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GL = (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (40 × 50.0) / 100 = 20.0 for a 150 g serving providing 50.0 g carbohydrate (33.33 g/100 g × 1.5).

hypertension.key_factor: Very high sugar content (30.2 g/100 g) independently elevates blood pressure risk via insulin-driven sodium retention and sympathetic nervous system activation, despite moderate sodium (109 mg/100 g).
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit chocolate sundae consumption; the 30.2 g/100 g sugar load independently raises blood pressure risk through insulin-mediated pathways regardless of the moderate sodium content, consistent with AHA 2018 dietary guidance on added sugars and hypertension.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar content of 30.2 g/100 g far exceeds the 20 g/100 g glycaemic ceiling for diabetes suitability; even a 150 g serving delivers approximately 45 g of sugar, causing significant postprandial blood glucose elevation.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid chocolate sundae; the very high sugar content (30.2 g/100 g) will cause significant postprandial blood glucose spikes, and even a small 150 g portion delivers approximately 45 g of sugar, well above safe limits for glycaemic management.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat (~3.8 g/100 g) combined with very high added sugar (30.2 g/100 g) raises LDL cholesterol and promotes endothelial inflammation, both established cardiovascular risk factors.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid or strictly limit chocolate sundae; the combination of approximately 3.8 g/100 g saturated fat and 30.2 g/100 g sugar contributes to elevated LDL cholesterol and systemic inflammation, consistent with AHA and ESC dietary guidelines for CVD risk reduction.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Very high refined sugar (30.2 g/100 g) and saturated fat (~3.8 g/100 g) promote pro-inflammatory mediators; no compensating anti-inflammatory nutrients are present.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid chocolate sundae, particularly during flares; the very high sugar and saturated fat content promotes inflammatory cytokine production with no offsetting anti-inflammatory nutrients such as omega-3 fatty acids, polyphenols, or antioxidants.

llm_training.natural_language_description: A chocolate sundae is a popular Western dessert consisting of soft serve ice cream dispensed into a cup or bowl and generously drizzled with hot chocolate fudge sauce. The soft serve base is made from a dairy mix of milk, cream, and sugar, churned at low temperature to produce a smooth, creamy, and slightly aerated texture. The hot fudge sauce is a thick, glossy chocolate topping made from cocoa, sugar, butter, and cream, which partially melts the surface of the ice cream on contact, creating a contrast of warm sauce and cold, creamy ice cream. The flavour profile is intensely sweet with rich chocolate notes and a smooth, melt-in-the-mouth texture. Per 100 g, the chocolate sundae provides 202 kcal, 4.0 g protein, 6.06 g fat (approximately 3.8 g saturated), and 33.33 g carbohydrate of which 30.2 g is sugar. Dietary fibre is 3.64 g/100 g, sodium is 109 mg/100 g, calcium is 98 mg/100 g, and iron is 0.6 mg/100 g. The glycemic index is approximately 40 (low GI), but the glycemic load per standard 150 g serving is approximately 20 (high), making it clinically significant for blood glucose management. The very high sugar content makes this food unsuitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, cardiovascular disease, or rheumatoid arthritis. It is best consumed as an occasional treat in small portions by healthy individuals. The food is vegetarian but not vegan, and contains dairy and potential gluten cross-contact allergens.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a chocolate sundae?
Is chocolate sundae high in sugar?
What is the GI of chocolate sundae?

How much fat is in a chocolate sundae?
What allergens are in a chocolate sundae?
Can I eat chocolate sundae if I have heart disease?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id F02020003, confidence 0.95, review_status reviewed). GI value of 40 is consistent with published University of Sydney GI Database range for chocolate ice cream. Saturated fat estimated at ~3.8 g/100 g based on USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry 19270 for comparable chocolate soft serve; this adaptation is documented in notes.
notes: Saturated fat (3.8 g/100 g) is estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry 19270 (Ice creams, chocolate) as the HPB Foodo source does not report saturated fat breakdown; this is an adapted value and is flagged accordingly. Fields set to -1 (monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g) are set to -1 because detailed fatty acid fractions, micronutrient panel, and moisture data for this specific chocolate sundae formulation are not reported in the HPB Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central for an exact match as of 2026. Calcium (98 mg/100 g) and iron (0.6 mg/100 g) are reported directly from HPB Foodo and retained. The input dietary_tag 'low_sodium' is noted but clinically the sugar content is the dominant concern. The input dietary_tag 'high_fibre' is noted; 3.64 g/100 g dietary fibre is above the 3 g/100 g threshold but does not offset the very high sugar content for diabetes or hypertension suitability. This food is not a Singaporean, Japanese, Korean, Indonesian, Thai, or Vietnamese dish; therefore no mandatory local-language names are required under G6 multilingual rules. The food is of Western/American origin and English is the sole mandatory language. GI glycemic load per serving recalculated: GL = (40 × 50.0) / 100 = 20.0 for a 150 g serving providing 50.0 g carbohydrate. FoodEx2 match_type corrected from 'broader' to 'closest_parent' to conform to the allowed enumeration values: closest_parent, exact, none.
